feature_name: Dual Operation Modes: Root Complex and End Point
description: The controller supports dual operation modes, functioning as either a Root Complex (RC) or an End Point (EP). Mode selection is via bootstrap pins PCIESSMODE[1:0] with software override in DEVSTAT. In RC mode it originates configuration accesses to downstream devices; in EP mode it responds as a PCIe endpoint. A single function is supported in EP mode. Design enables flexible system roles without silicon changes.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; Section 1.4.1 PCI Express Core Module; Section 2.11 Operations
dependencies: Power domain enabled; PCIESSMODE[1:0] strapped; LTSSM_EN sequencing
Remarks: Choose the mode early in boot; if software overrides bootstrap, ensure downstream topology and BARs are re-initialized before enabling link.

description: A single bidirectional link supports up to two lanes with dynamic-width conversion. Link operates at 2.5 GT/s (Gen1) or 5.0 GT/s (Gen2). Automatic lane reversal and polarity inversion on receive are supported. Ultra-low TX/RX latency is targeted by the core. The design cannot split a x2 link into two x1 ports.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; LINK_CAP[MAX_LINK_WIDTH, MAX_LINK_SPEED]; SERDES_CFGx[RX_INVPAIR, TX_INVPAIR]
dependencies: SerDes PLL locked; PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD/LN_EN; PL_LINK_CTRL.LNK_MODE; Proper refclk
Remarks: For Gen2 bring-up, set PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD after Gen1 link-up; verify negotiated width via LINK_STAT_CTRL[NEGOTIATED_LINK_WD].

feature_name: CPU-Side Interfaces: 128-bit VBUSM Master and Slave
description: The PCIe subsystem exposes one 128-bit VBUSM master port and one 128-bit VBUSM slave port to the SoC interconnect. The master port services inbound (PCIe-initiated) transactions to internal memory. The slave port is used for PCIe MMR accesses and outbound data transfers. Both ports are active in RC and EP modes.
evidence: Section 1.4.3 VBUSM (Configuration and DMA Access Interface)
dependencies: Interconnect availability; Bus clock valid; OB/IB translation configured
Remarks: Ensure bus QoS and arbitration meet ~85% link throughput per lane as noted in DMA guidance.

description: SerDes includes an internal PLL that multiplies the differential reference clock to required line rates. Configuration uses PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL to set MPY, loop bandwidth, and enable (ENPLL). Lock status is read via PCIE_SERDES_STS[LOCK] with per-lane test/status bits. Reference clock jitter and common clock usage impact link training performance.
evidence: Section 2.3 SerDes Configuration; Registers PCIE_SERDES_CFGPLL (0x02620358), PCIE_SERDES_STS (0x0262015C)
dependencies: Stable refclk; Proper MPY based on refclk; Allow 1µs regulator settle; LOCK=1 before transactions
Remarks: Match MPY per Table 2-4; when using SSC, set common clock semantics via LINK_STAT_CTRL[COMMON_CLK_CFG] and train with increased TS count if needed.

description: The PCIESS uses a differential PHY reference clock and an internal VBUSM clock from the SoC PLL. Common clock architecture is supported and recommended especially when SSC is present. If no common clock is used, software must clear COMMON_CLK_CFG and expect longer training sequences. PCIe operation depends on SerDes PLL lock before any register access.
evidence: Section 2.2 Clock Control; LINK_STAT_CTRL[COMMON_CLK_CFG]
dependencies: Refclk meeting PCIe CEM; COMMON_CLK_CFG set appropriately; SerDes LOCK=1
Remarks: When using backplanes with SSC, enable common clock at both ends and ensure ACK/NFTS timing fields suit the clocking profile.

description: Two internal address spaces are defined. Address Space 0 (16 KB) maps application registers, local config, remote config, and (RC only) a 4 KB I/O window. Address Space 1 is used for data transfers and may map to multiple remote devices in RC mode. Accesses to these windows must be 32-bit and non-cacheable; no bursts on AS0.
evidence: Section 2.8 PCIe Address Spaces; Figure 2-5; Section 2.8.1.1 Caution
dependencies: BAR0 mapping for AS0; IOBASE set for RC I/O; Non-cacheable region attributes
Remarks: Guard remote config and IO windows; block accesses when link is down to avoid hanging transactions.

description: Outbound translation maps internal bus addresses to PCIe memory addresses using 32 equally sized regions. Region size is set by OB_SIZE (1,2,4,8 MB) and per-region mapping by OB_OFFSET_INDEXn/OB_OFFSETn_HI. Only memory (not config/IO) TLPs use outbound translation. Transactions must not cross a translation boundary.
evidence: Section 2.7.1; Registers OB_SIZE, OB_OFFSET_INDEXn, OB_OFFSETn_HI; Example 2-1
dependencies: CMD_STATUS[OB_XLT_EN]=1; Regions enabled (OB_OFFSET_INDEXn[ENABLE]); Proper sizing
Remarks: Design regions to avoid boundary crossing for bursts; mask higher address bits appropriately per OB_SIZE index extraction.

description: Inbound translation remaps accepted PCIe addresses to internal memory using up to 4 regions. Each region associates to a BAR via IB_BARn and defines start address and offset using IB_STARTn_LO/HI and IB_OFFSETn. BAR0 is reserved for application registers and cannot be remapped. Special handling allows BAR1's value as start when IB_START is zero.
evidence: Section 2.7.2; Registers IB_BARn, IB_STARTn_LO/HI, IB_OFFSETn; Sections 2.7.2.2 and 2.7.2.3
dependencies: CMD_STATUS[IB_XLT_EN]=1; BARs configured; IB regions programmed; EP/RC addressing width consistent
Remarks: Keep start addresses ordered if mapping non-contiguous ranges with one region; ensure TLPs do not cross 4 KB boundaries as per PCIe rules.

description: BAR Mask registers allow software to predefine BAR sizes/types by overlaying BARs before enumeration. Writes require enabling CMD_STATUS[DBI_CS2], and must be cleared after configuration. BAR0 mask is limited (bits [31:20]) because BAR0 maps application registers in both RC and EP.
evidence: Section 2.7.3.1 BAR Mask Registers; CMD_STATUS[DBI_CS2]; Type0/1 BARx_MASK
dependencies: Enable DBI_CS2; Write BARx_MASK then program BAR; Clear DBI_CS2 and verify by readback tests
Remarks: Do not modify BAR masks from the serial link side; use internal bus and test patterns to confirm masking worked as expected.

feature_name: External DMA Support via EDMA (No Built-in DMA)
description: The PCIESS has no internal DMA engine but supports high-throughput transfers using an external EDMA. In RC mode, EDMA can move data to/from remote devices; in EP mode, EDMA can move between internal memory and mapped PCIe windows. Approximate sustainable bandwidth is ~85% of effective line rate per lane in each direction.
evidence: Section 2.9 DMA Support; Examples 2-4 and 2-5
dependencies: EDMA configured; Outbound/inbound translations set; Bus master enabled; BARs mapped
Remarks: Chunk transfers to the configured Max Payload; align to 128 B and avoid misaligned bursts to prevent splitting and performance loss.

feature_name: TLP Attributes: Relaxed Ordering and No Snoop
description: The controller can tag outgoing TLPs with Relaxed Ordering and/or No Snoop attributes. TLPCFG provides global enable for these attributes. Additionally, DEV_STAT_CTRL contains per-function controls for error reporting and ordering behavior. Disabling relaxed ordering in RC may be necessary to preserve software expectations on ordering.
evidence: Registers TLPCFG[RELAXED, NO_SNOOP]; DEV_STAT_CTRL[RELAXED, NO_SNOOP]
dependencies: Driver policy; Platform coherency and interconnect settings
Remarks: Use relaxed ordering only when end-to-end consumers tolerate reordering; disable for MSI/MEM writes that signal completion dependencies.

description: While the PCIe core is little-endian at pins, the subsystem supports system big-endian with programmable swap granularity. ENDIAN[1:0] selects swap on 1-, 2-, 4-, or 8-byte for outbound and inbound paths. Big-endian byte swap behavior is detailed for inbound/outbound, ensuring FBE/LBE reflect true transfer size.
evidence: Section 2.10.2 Support for Endian Modes; Tables 2-8 and 2-9; Register ENDIAN
dependencies: Device boot endianness; ENDIAN register set consistently; Software buffer alignment
Remarks: Verify swap mode against peripheral data structures; for MMR (32-bit) accesses use 32-bit boundary swaps regardless of data bus width.

description: In RC mode, the controller receives legacy INTA–INTD messages and exposes status/enable per line. In EP mode, legacy assertion/deassertion can be generated using EP_IRQ_SET/CLR if MSI is disabled. Legacy reception requires software to probe downstream status and handle deassert race with EOI.
evidence: Section 2.14.1 and 2.14.5.1; Registers LEGACY_[A..D]_*, EP_IRQ_SET/CLR, IRQ_EOI
dependencies: INTx enabled in config space; LEGACY_* enables set; Proper EOI writes
Remarks: Read downstream INTx statuses before EOI to avoid retrigger due to late deassert; prefer MSI where possible.

feature_name: MSI Generation and Reception (32 Vectors Grouped)
description: MSI is supported in both RC and EP roles with up to 32 vectors. Generation uses a 32-bit memory write to the MSI address with MSI data (vector encoding). Reception maps groups of four vectors to MSI0–MSI7 register blocks with enable/status and raw-status. An MSI_IRQ doorbell register in BAR0 space is used for remote-triggered MSI to RC/EP.
evidence: Section 2.14.2 and 2.14.5.2; Registers MSI_CAP, MSI_LOW32/UP32, MSI_DATA; MSI_IRQ; MSI[0..7]_IRQ_*
dependencies: MSI_CAP[MSI_EN]=1; Address/Data programmed by RC; BAR0 reachability; IRQ_EOI usage
Remarks: After servicing, write 1 to MSIn_IRQ_STATUS bit and then write IRQ_EOI with the event number to re-arm; ensure INTx disabled when MSI enabled.

description: PCI-compatible error bits (STATUS_COMMAND) mirror poisoning and aborts for legacy software. Baseline PCIe Device Status and Control (DEV_STAT_CTRL) enables reporting of Correctable, Non-Fatal, Fatal, and Unsupported Request errors. Link-related retraining can be initiated via LINK_STAT_CTRL[RETRAIN_LINK] (RC only).
evidence: Section 2.16.2.1 and 2.16.2.2; Registers STATUS_COMMAND, DEV_STAT_CTRL, LINK_STAT_CTRL
dependencies: Error reporting enables set; System error routing configured; RC-only bits gated in EP
Remarks: Set only the reporting enables you intend to service; clear sticky status by writing 1 and log context promptly to avoid loss of first error.

description: AER is implemented with uncorrectable/correctable status, masks, and severity controls. ECRC generation and checking are supported and controlled in PCIE_ACCR. Header logs (HDR_LOG0–3) capture TLP headers for selected errors. In RC, ROOT_ERR_CMD/ROOT_ERR_ST and ERR_SRC_ID track and route error messages to software.
evidence: Section 2.16.2.3; Registers PCIE_UNCERR, PCIE_UNCERR_MASK, PCIE_UNCERR_SVRTY, PCIE_CERR, PCIE_CERR_MASK, PCIE_ACCR, ROOT_ERR_CMD, ROOT_ERR_ST, ERR_SRC_ID
dependencies: AER enables set; PME/interrupt routing; Ensure peer devices' ECRC capability
Remarks: Disable ECRC (PCIE_ACCR[ECRC_CHK_EN, ECRC_GEN_EN]=0) if peers lack support, especially when this device is RC to avoid false errors.

description: The function supports D0, optional D1/D2, and D3hot/D3cold states under PMCAP/PM_CTL_STAT controls. PME generation from selected states is programmable in PMCAP and enabled via PM_CTL_STAT[PME_EN]. Device in D3hot may return to D0 with or without internal reset as configured. D3cold requires re-initialization.
evidence: Section 2.15.1 Device Power Management; Registers PMCAP, PM_CTL_STAT
dependencies: PME routing enabled in RC; Software coordinates quiesce before transitions; Power/clock available for D3hot
Remarks: Set NO_SOFT_RST when needed to preserve state across D3hot→D0; validate PME support bits align with driver expectations.

description: ASPM L0s and L1 are supported with transitions controlled by HW timers and optional software. LINK_CAP[AS_LINK_PM] advertises support; LINK_STAT_CTRL[ACTIVE_LINK_PM] enables entry. ACK/NFTS and L0s/L1 timing and entry latencies are tuned via ACK_FREQ and SYM_NUM registers for stable recovery.
evidence: Section 2.15.2; Registers LINK_CAP, LINK_STAT_CTRL, ACK_FREQ, SYM_NUM
dependencies: Both ends advertise compatible ASPM; Common clock considerations; Timers configured
Remarks: Tune L0s/L1 entry and NFTs to platform traces to balance power savings against recovery latency; verify DLL active behavior across transitions.

feature_name: Reset: POR, System, and Software Hot Reset
description: Power-on reset resets the PCIESS; system hard/soft resets reset PCIESS with sticky MMR bits preserved on soft reset. Software hot reset is initiated via RSTCMD[INIT_RST] downstream and requires flushing pending transactions (RSTCMD[FLUSH_N]=0) and 100 ms delay before new traffic.
evidence: Section 2.13 Reset Considerations; Register RSTCMD
dependencies: RSTCMD sequencing; Ensure no pending traffic; Observe 100 ms/1.5 s timeouts
Remarks: De-assert CMD_STATUS[LTSSM_EN] before hot reset; re-run initialization including PLL and link training after reset completion.

description: The controller supports PIPE (link-layer) loopback for symbol-level testing with test equipment. As loopback master, enable PL_LINK_CTRL[LPBK_EN] and retrain (RC) or force recovery (EP). As loopback slave, inbound symbols are looped back without additional programming. TLP-level loopback is not supported in PIPE mode.
evidence: Section 2.12.1 PIPE Loopback; PL_LINK_CTRL[LPBK_EN]; LINK_STAT_CTRL[RETRAIN_LINK]; PL_FORCE_LINK
dependencies: Link established; Test equipment coordination; Correct mode (RC/EP)
Remarks: Use only with protocol testers; this is not a data path validation for TLP transactions.

description: PHY-level loopback feeds transmitted data back into receiver at the PHY, enabling TLP loopback without a partner. Configure SERDES_CFGx[TX_LOOPBACK=2/3, RX_LOOPBACK=3], disable RX_LOS as noted, then force LTSSM to POLL_ACTIVE with PL_FORCE_LINK. BAR and Base/Limit ranges must not absorb the test traffic.
evidence: Section 2.12.2 PHY Loopback; Registers SERDES_CFG0/1, PL_FORCE_LINK
dependencies: RC mode; Loopback set before any traffic; PL_GEN2/LINK control access
Remarks: Ensure BAR0/BAR1 and bridge Base/Limit ranges do not capture test TLP addresses; otherwise looped traffic will not reach the master interface.

description: RC bring-up includes refclk and PSC enable, selecting RC mode, programming SerDes PLL, waiting for LOCK, disabling LTSSM, programming config registers, enabling LTSSM, checking LTSSM_STATE=0x11 (L0), and then enumerating the bus. After enumeration, set up DMA, interrupts, and address translation.
evidence: Section 2.11.1.1 Initialization Sequence (RC); DEBUG0[LTSSM_STATE]
dependencies: PSC power domain ON; SerDes LOCK; CMD_STATUS[LTSSM_EN] sequencing; Proper config of BARs, OB/IB
Remarks: Automate state checks via DEBUG0; only start config/IO accesses after L0 is observed and link partner is ready.

description: EP bring-up mirrors RC, with EP mode selection, PLL configuration and lock wait, LTSSM disable/program/enable, and L0 confirmation. If more time is needed before RC config, enable CMD_STATUS[APP_RETRY_EN] to return RETRY for accesses until ready. After configuration is complete, allow DMA and interrupts.
evidence: Section 2.11.2.1 Initialization Sequence (EP); CMD_STATUS[APP_RETRY_EN]
dependencies: PSC power domain ON; SerDes LOCK; APP_RETRY_EN used only during init; LTSSM L0
Remarks: Clear APP_RETRY_EN once ready to avoid indefinite RETRY; coordinate BARs and inbound windows before enabling bus mastering.

description: DEBUG0/DEBUG1 registers expose LTSSM state, PIPE activity, training and idle conditions, and skip status. Appendix A maps LTSSM encoded values to textual states from DETECT_QUIET to HOT_RESET. These allow firmware to instrument link training and error recovery paths. TX compliance, detect, and resets are also visible.
evidence: Registers DEBUG0, DEBUG1; Appendix A Table A-1
dependencies: Readable after clocks and PLL lock; Link active for meaningful states
Remarks: Poll DEBUG0[LTSSM_STATE] during bring-up and failure cases; use with PL_LINK_CTRL/PL_FORCE_LINK to correlate forced transitions.

feature_name: Transaction Size, Alignment, and Byte Strobes
description: Outbound transactions are limited to 128 bytes and must be 8-byte aligned; misaligned writes reduce maximum size to 120 bytes. Byte enables must be contiguous ones (no holes). Read interleaving does not occur if read sizes stay within Max Read Request (typically 128 bytes). These constraints optimize bridge behavior and prevent unspecified outcomes.
dependencies: DMA/programmed I/O adheres to sizes; MRRS/MPS set appropriately
Remarks: Configure MRRS/MPS in config space consistently with driver I/O patterns; enforce contiguous strobes in software writers.

feature_name: Port Link Control and Directed Gen2 Speed Change
description: Port Link Control (PL_LINK_CTRL) manages DLL enable, loopback, scrambler, hot reset, and link mode. Gen2 Register (PL_GEN2) supports directed speed change via DIR_SPD and de-emphasis/FTS control. Use LINK_STAT_CTRL fields to observe negotiated speed and DLL Active. These provide precise link behavior control during bring-up and tuning.
evidence: Registers PL_LINK_CTRL, PL_GEN2, LINK_STAT_CTRL
dependencies: LTSSM disabled before mode changes; Common clock settings consistent; Partner capability for Gen2
Remarks: After reaching L0 at Gen1, set PL_GEN2.DIR_SPD=1 to initiate Gen2; verify LINK_STAT_CTRL[LINK_SPEED]=2 and re-tune FTS if needed.

description: Filter/mask registers allow relaxing or enforcing completion attribute/length/TC/function/tag checks. They also govern vendor messages, DLLP aborts, and flush handling. Symbol timing controls (SKP_VALUE, timers) are available to adjust SKP emission and watchdog behavior for robustness in diverse channels.
evidence: Registers SYMTIMER_FLTMASK, FLT_MASK2, SYM_NUM (3.9.9–3.9.10; 3.9.8)
dependencies: Use only when interoperability is validated; Defaults follow PCIe spec
Remarks: Keep default strict checks unless interoperability testing dictates relaxation; document any deviations for compliance audits.

description: The module complies with PCI Express Base Specification Revision 2.0 and integrates a Synopsys DWC PCIe Dual Core (v3.51a) with TI SerDes 1.1.03. Not supported features include using x2 as two x1 links, multiple VCs/TCs, function-level reset, beacon in-band wake, hot-plug hardware, inbound IO in RC/EP, non-incremental bursts, auxiliary power for D3cold, and L2 link state. These constraints shape system design choices.
evidence: Section 1.6 Industry Standard(s) Compliance Statement; Section 1.3 Features (unsupported list)
dependencies: System requirements aligned to Gen2, x2 max; System-level hot-plug handled externally if needed
Remarks: Plan topology and power-management strategies with these limitations; avoid relying on L2 or hardware hot-plug features.

description: Transaction Priority Register configures master privilege mode and PRIVID for inbound master transactions on the internal bus. It also sets priority level (0 highest to 7 lowest) per inbound transaction. This enables basic access control and traffic shaping towards system resources.
evidence: Register PRIORITY (3.1.14) [MST_PRIV, MST_PRIVID, MST_PRIORITY]
dependencies: Interconnect honors PRIVID/priority; Supervisor/user mode semantics defined
Remarks: Use supervisor mode for register accesses; assign PRIVID consistently with SoC firewall and QoS policies.

description: An IRQ_EOI register accepts the interrupt event number to signal end-of-interrupt. MSI and legacy groups map to interrupt event numbers per Table 2-10. Clearing MSIn_IRQ_STATUS with write-1 and then issuing appropriate EOI is required to fully de-assert and re-arm the interrupt line towards the CPU interrupt controller.
evidence: Registers IRQ_EOI (3.1.15), MSI0–MSI7 IRQ blocks; Section 2.14.1/Table 2-10
dependencies: Correct event-number mapping; Write-1-to-clear semantics observed
Remarks: Invoke EOI only after clearing status bits; maintain a mapping from MSI vector to CPU IRQs for deterministic service.

description: Automatic lane reversal is supported to ease routing, and per-lane polarity inversion is configurable in SerDes. RX_INVPAIR and TX_INVPAIR swap signal polarity if differential pairs are flipped. These features improve board flexibility without sacrificing protocol correctness when negotiated during training.
evidence: Section 1.3 Features; Registers SERDES_CFG0/1 [RX_INVPAIR, TX_INVPAIR]
dependencies: Proper SerDes config; Training succeeds with partner; Signal integrity within spec
Remarks: Prefer automatic negotiation; only force inversion when board routing requires it and SI validation is complete.

feature_name: Dynamic Width Conversion and Lane Enable
description: The PHY performs data bus width conversion between Gen1 and Gen2 while keeping PLL at 2.5 GHz. Lane enable fields in PL_GEN2 and LNK_MODE in PL_LINK_CTRL select 1 or 2 lanes. Hardware automatically manages width negotiation per partner capabilities. This enables flexible link upsizing while maintaining clocking simplicity.
evidence: Section 2.3.3 Note; Registers PL_GEN2[LN_EN], PL_LINK_CTRL[LNK_MODE]; LINK_STAT_CTRL[NEGOTIATED_LINK_WD]
dependencies: Partner supports target width; PLL configured to 2.5 GHz; DIR_SPD for Gen2
Remarks: Verify negotiated width after training and adjust lane enables to match board strapping; do not assume x2 on all platforms.

description: The controller transmits and receives PCIe Message TLPs. Software can send a specific message by writing payload to PL_OMSG and toggling PL_LINK_CTRL[OMSG_REQ]. Power management messages (PM_TURNOFF/PM_PME) are issued through PMCMD, and related events are reported in PMRST IRQ status registers.
evidence: Registers PL_OMSG and PL_LINK_CTRL[OMSG_REQ] (3.9.3/3.9.6); PMCMD (3.1.8); PMRST_* (3.1.76–3.1.79)
dependencies: Link active; Message routing permitted by fabric; PM capabilities negotiated
Remarks: Throttle software-initiated messages to avoid flooding; treat PM messages per role (RC vs EP) as defined in spec.

feature_name: Power Domain and Module State Transitions (PSC)
description: Turning off the PCIe power domain/module state resets PCIESS and requires re-initialization. All inbound/outbound traffic must be quiesced on both local and remote devices before power-gating to avoid system hang. After power-up, follow normal PLL and LTSSM initialization; no extra delay is required beyond PSC completion.
evidence: Section 2.13.3 Power Domain and Module State Transitions Considerations
dependencies: PSC transition sequencing; Traffic drained; Software coordination with peers
Remarks: Poll PSC GOSTAT and ensure link is idle via ACT_STATUS; notify remote to stop traffic before gating power.
